comments.author: @timvines7944
comments.text: Beautiful video.  It’s a shame that we tend to want to worship the creation rather than the creator.  The living God of the universe indeed created all things.  His creation is beautiful and it did not take millions or billions of years to do it but, only 6 days.  This is something only God could have done. 🙏🏻

comments.author: @OliveJuice-u6o
comments.text: And we love it ,80 acres of our families farm runs along part of the preserved Appalachian Trail. They are up a few ridges sometimes we see them hikers on rocks. We’re familiar with sharp top dragons. tooth . We don’t need trails , we have volunteerered for search. We usually go horseback we spotlight for lost hikers. we know the back ways and don’t take trails, but these are our hollows . We know the land we’ve left the land. We’ve gone to the big cities. We’ve got an educations. We’ve always come back because these are a mountains that raised our great grandma’s babies and her babies and her babies and our babies. Those hikers from the outside lost their way on the trails . We think sometimes they must’ve left them , you know, there are caves that have drop down points from above. Sometimes we think animals like bear or bobcat maybe or it could be the rivers , streams and waterfalls . We have conspiracies and hear rumors . Sometimes we think men might have not wanted to be found. If that’s the case they could do that for a lifetime. It’s beautiful. We hear you at stores sometimes. So confident, we wish you the best. Sometimes a person’s confidence is also what causes downfalls. You get so high the air is different, you can sweat in the morning and freeze at night. New species are still found. You know ppl still live off grid on purpose before it was called a lifestyle.

replies.author: @trueglobe66

comments.author: @alphariggins9449
comments.text:
I have been in the area that is off limits and there is an area where, although very overgrown in areas you can clearly see the Stone structure s similar to what I have seen at Chitchinetzu in Mexico. .  I made some simple sketches and began some layouts and measurements prior to going to the site in Mexico.  Accordi ng to the Geologist who came and did a show about the site it's the only known source for a specific Pigment of Blue which is only found in the regions surrounding this site.  it is also the same Blue of the Pict Tribes 
Of the North Sea used for their war paint.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: That’s a fascinating detail — I hadn’t heard about that pigment connection before. Appalachia has so many hidden layers of history, geology, and old stories that rarely get talked about. Do you remember the name of the show or the geologist who covered that site?

comments.author: @Kel-d7v
comments.text:
I've lived in the Blue Ridge Mountains since 1978-79.
"There's no place like home."
I've never lived more than 5 miles away from the Blue Ridge Parkway my entire life, and have spent SO much of my time driving up and down that road. The Appalachian Trail is one of the best places to go when the summer days are just too hot to take because it's always cooler and breezy up in the mountains. I would grab a drink and a snack and just walk ..  and walk ... and walk ..... 🫠
* this video doesn't even begin to show the world what these mountains are really like. There is SO much more.
